mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 8% triggers a labor market signal that reduces consumer spending and boosts equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 8% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 90 triggers a supply chain signal that reduces inventory turnover and boosts equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 90 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.2 million triggers a demand signal that boosts consumer spending and reduces equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above 150,000 triggers a labor market signal that reduces consumer spending and boosts equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening signal that boosts discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 3.5% triggers a demand signal that boosts consumer spending and reduces equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 95 triggers a supply chain signal that reduces inventory turnover and boosts equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 95 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
